examples.source:
spec 'tour values and immutable updates'
  expect stdout "Ada has 2 visits\n[\"Ada\",\"Grace\",\"Katherine\"]\nAda still has 1 visit\n"
end

program
  let user = {name: 'Ada', visits: 1}
  let next = {...user, visits: user.visits + 1}
  let names = ['Ada', 'Grace']
  let expanded = [...names, 'Katherine']

  says next.name + ' has ' + next.visits + ' visits'
  says expanded.to_json
  says user.name + ' still has ' + user.visits + ' visit'
end
examples.title: Values and immutable updates

examples.source:
spec 'tour functions and callable values'
  expect stdout "Hello, Ada!\n42\n"
end

fn greet(name)
  return "Hello, #{name}!"
end

fn add(a, b)
  return a + b
end

program
  let formatter = greet
  let operations = [add]
  says formatter('Ada')
  let operation = operations[0]
  says operation(40, 2)
end
examples.title: Functions and callable values

examples.source:
spec 'tour actors own state'
  expect stdout "visits=1\nvisits=2\n"
end

actor Counter(label)
  state count = 0
  receive message

  if message == 'increment'
    set count = count + 1
    says label + '=' + count
  else
    if message == 'stop'
      exit
    end
  end
end

program
  let counter = spawn Counter('visits')
  send counter, 'increment'
  send counter, 'increment'
  send counter, 'stop'
  join counter
end
examples.title: Actors and private state

examples.source:
spec 'tour stateless worker pools'
  expect stdout "30\ncompleted=4\n"
end

worker Square(message)
  send message.reply_to, message.value * message.value
end

actor Total(expected)
  state seen = 0
  state total = 0
  receive value

  set seen = seen + 1
  set total = total + value
  if seen == expected
    says total
    exit
  end
end

program
  let collector = spawn Total(4)
  let pool = workers(Square, count: 2, capacity: 8)

  for value in [1, 2, 3, 4]
    if !pool.enqueue({reply_to: collector, value: value})
      fail 'tour worker queue is full'
    end
  end

  join collector
  says 'completed=' + pool.status().completed
  let stopped = pool.stop()
end
examples.title: Stateless worker pools

examples.source:
spec 'tour jobs add receipts and results'
  expect stdout "thumbs/cover.jpg\ncompleted=1\n"
end

worker Thumbnail(image)
  return {path: 'thumbs/' + image.id + '.jpg'}
end

program
  let Thumbnails = Jobs.define('TourThumbnails', Thumbnail, count: 2, capacity: 8)
  let receipt = Thumbnails.enqueue({id: 'cover'})
  if !receipt.accepted
    fail 'tour job queue is full'
  end

  let result = Jobs.result(Thumbnails, receipt, 5000)
  if !result.ok
    fail result.error
  end

  says result.value.path
  says 'completed=' + Thumbnails.status().completed
  let stopped = Thumbnails.stop()
end
examples.title: Jobs, receipts, and results

examples.source:
spec 'tour bounded parallel streams'
  expect stdout "6,8\n"
end

import 'stdlib:Streams'

fn double(value)
  return value * 2
end

fn greater_than_four(value)
  return value > 4
end

program
  let values = [1, 2, 3, 4]
    .map(double, workers: 2)
    .filter(greater_than_four, workers: 2)
    .collect()

  says join(values, ',')
end
examples.title: Bounded parallel streams

examples.source:
spec 'tour explicit io handles and result values'
  expect stdout "hello from a file\nclosed=true\n"
end

import 'stdlib:Bytes'
import 'stdlib:Files'
import 'stdlib:Io'

program
  let path = 'build/tour-io.txt'
  let writer = Files.open(path, 'write').value
  let wrote = writer.write_all("hello from a file\n")
  let writer_closed = writer.close()

  let reader = Files.open(path, 'read').value
  let line = reader.read_line(128)
  says Bytes.decode(line.value.data, 'utf8')
  says 'closed=' + reader.close().ok
end
examples.title: I/O handles and Result values

examples.source:
spec 'tour web routes remain ordinary callables'
  expect stdout "200\nHello, Ada\n"
end

import 'stdlib:Web'

fn hello(request)
  return Web.text('Hello, ' + request.params.name)
end

program
  let routes = Web.routes([
    Web.get('/hello/:name', hello)
  ])
  let response = Web.dispatch(routes, {
    method: 'GET',
    path: '/hello/Ada',
    headers: {},
    body: ''
  })

  says response.status
  says response.body
end
examples.title: Web routes as ordinary values
